state.output:
<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/anobel.com/004-bento-visuals/research/inputs/gen-tile.mjs</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: import fs from 'node:fs';
2: const auth = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(process.env.HOME+'/.local/share/opencode/auth.json','utf8'));
3: const key = auth['zai-coding-plan'].key;
4: const spec = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(process.argv[2],'utf8'));
5: const img = fs.readFileSync(spec.refImage).toString('base64');
6: const EP='https://api.z.ai/api/coding/paas/v4/chat/completions';
7: 
8: function contract(title,desc,treatment){return `You are GLM-5.2, a multimodal model that CAN see attached images. An image IS attached and visible to you — do NOT deliberate about whether you can see it; you can. Even if you somehow cannot, the LAYOUT section below fully specifies the shell, so build from that — never refuse or explain, just output the HTML.
9: 
10: You are a senior product-UI engineer. The attached image is the EMPTY reference shell for a Dutch maritime "Vloot-functie" bento tile (title "${title}", description "${desc}"). Build ONE self-contained 560×480 HTML tile in the EXACT house style below, filling the body with this treatment:
11: 
12: TREATMENT: ${treatment}
13: 
14: OUTPUT: a single complete HTML document only — no markdown fences, no prose. Inline <style>, inline SVG, the Hanken Grotesk Google Fonts link only.
15: 
16: LAYOUT (match the reference shell exactly):
17: - Card 560×480, page bg #eceef0, centered; background #ffffff, border-radius 22px (NOT >=24), NO border, box-shadow:0 1px 2px rgba(20,28,46,.04),0 26px 52px -22px rgba(20,28,46,.20), padding 30px, overflow hidden; font 'Hanken Grotesk',Arial,sans-serif; -webkit-font-smoothing:antialiased; font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums; color #0a1a2f.
18: - TITLE + DESCRIPTION SIT AT THE BOTTOM-LEFT of the card (exactly like the reference shell). Title "${title}" 21px weight 700 #0a1a2f. Description "${desc}" 13.5px weight 500 #4e4e4e, directly under the title.
19: - The instrument/content fills the body ABOVE the title block (a floating inner panel: #fefefe + 1px #ececec border + tight <=14px-blur shadow, radius <=16).
20: - Eyebrow chip TOP-RIGHT: a small ANCHOR icon (maritime) + the text "Vloot-functie" (Title case, NOT uppercase), brand blue #06458c, on a #f6f8fc rounded pill. Use ONLY this anchor icon for the eyebrow — no other glyph.
21: - Circular ghost ↗ button BOTTOM-RIGHT (34px, white, 1px #e2e2e2).
22: - EVERYTHING must fit inside the 560×480 card — nothing clipped or overflowing. Size the instrument to leave room for the bottom title+desc block.
23: 
24: Spend boldness on ONE signature instrument moment for the treatment, grounded in maritime-B2B fleet procurement; everything else quiet. Realistic Dutch data (ships MS Aldebaran/Castor/Pollux/Vesta; maart 2026). Format euro amounts consistently as €X.XXX (thousands dot, no cents).
25: 
26: Palette ONLY (zero out-of-palette hex): brand #06458c/#053b77/#043367; green #367e39 (status/success only); red #c9140f (alerts only); text #0a1a2f; muted secondary/meta/caption/table-header TEXT must be #4e4e4e or #0a1a2f, NEVER #787878 and NEVER #8591b3/#acb3c9/#cbd0dc as TEXT color (they fail WCAG AA) — those light neutrals are for borders, dividers, dots, strokes and fills ONLY; neutrals #ececec #e7e9ee #cbd0dc #8591b3 #acb3c9 #f6f8fc #fefefe #ffffff #e2e2e2. NO orange. No gradient except optionally the navy. No glassmorphism. Reduced-motion-guard any hover. Status by icon+word not color alone. Return ONLY the HTML.`;}
27: 
28: async function gen(t){
29:   if(fs.existsSync(t.out)){ try{ if(/<\/html>\s*$/i.test(fs.readFileSync(t.out,"utf8"))) return `SKIP ${t.n}`; }catch{} }
30:   const body={model:'glm-5.2',max_tokens:24000,temperature:0.4,thinking:{type:'disabled'},messages:[{role:'user',content:[
31:     {type:'text',text:contract(spec.title,spec.desc,t.brief)},
32:     {type:'image_url',image_url:{url:'data:image/png;base64,'+img}}]}]};
33:   for(let a=1;a<=5;a++){
34:     const ac=new AbortController(); const to=setTimeout(()=>ac.abort(),150000);
35:     try{
36:       const r=await fetch(EP,{method:'POST',headers:{'Authorization':'Bearer '+key,'Content-Type':'application/json'},body:JSON.stringify(body),signal:ac.signal});
37:       clearTimeout(to);
38:       const j=await r.json();
39:       let h=(j.choices?.[0]?.message?.content||'').replace(/^```(?:html)?\s*/i,'').replace(/\s*```\s*$/,'').trim();
40:       const fin=j.choices?.[0]?.finish_reason;
41:       if((h.toLowerCase().startsWith('<!doctype')||h.toLowerCase().startsWith('<html')) && /<\/html>\s*$/i.test(h)){ fs.writeFileSync(t.out,h); return `OK ${t.n} (${h.length}b, ${fin})`; }
42:       if(a===5) return `FAIL ${t.n} finish=${fin} len=${h.length} (incomplete/no-html)`;
43:     }catch(e){ clearTimeout(to); if(a===5) return `ERR ${t.n} ${e.message}`; }
44:     await new Promise(s=>setTimeout(s,5000*a));
45:   }
46: }
47: for(const t of spec.treatments){ console.log(new Date().toISOString().slice(11,19), await gen(t)); }
48: console.log('DONE');

(End of file - total 48 lines)
</content>
state.title: .opencode/specs/anobel.com/004-bento-visuals/research/inputs/gen-tile.mjs

part.text:
## Verdict
**AGREE-WITH-CHANGES — confidence 0.70** — mechanism is sound in principle, but the cost/benefit vs. a single tighter-prompt round-1 regen is unproven, and three load-bearing assumptions (GLM round-2 instruction compliance, MiniMax rescore stability, audit finding accuracy) are asserted, not verified.

## What GPT-5.5 got right
- **Generator ≠ auditor is settled correctly** — GLM-5.2 confabulation in vision-audit-benchmark.md §3 is real; an external critique channel is required.
- **Adopt-if multi-gate shape is right** — combining proof_check + contrast_check + geometry_check + MiniMax rescore is the correct defense against false-fixes (REQ-004).
- **Failure-only routing is the correct default** — skipping the 27 SHIP sentinels is the right cost discipline; the "SKIP_SHIP" early-exit is clean.
- **Hard predecessor gate on phase 001** is correct — without the deterministic surface, adopt_if cannot block overflow/contrast false-fixes.
- **T0 vs. T1 A/B framing** is the right experimental instinct — a comparator arm proves the structure earns its place.

## Gaps / risks / errors
1. **GLM round-2 compliance on instructions round-1 ignored is the load-bearing assumption that breaks the whole phase.** Round-1's contract (`gen-tile.mjs:22`) already says "Size the instrument to leave room for the bottom title+desc block" — GLM ignored it on tile 4. Round-2's "use linear/stacked/bounded-lane primitives" is the same kind of instruction. **RC-3 root cause is partly "instruction-density mismatch"** — A4 ADDS a typed-JSON block on top, increasing prompt density further. The 5-7 conversion prediction has no empirical basis if round-2 compliance matches round-1 compliance.
2. **Cheaper alternative not considered: a single augmented-prompt round-1 regen.** Extract the 18 issues verbatim → 5-8 distilled rules → re-run `gen-tile.mjs` on the 18 FIX tiles with a 10-line prompt addition. No adapter, no JSON schema, no T0/T1 arm, no T0-vs-T1 cost. Same +1-3 SHIP potential at ~1.0x marginal cost (just the 18 extra GLM calls), vs. A4's 1.5-2x (18 round-2 GLM + 18 MiniMax rescore).
3. **`classifyFixes` regex is fragile on real audit text.** Sampled `audit-accountbeheer.json`: tile-2's "introduces an off-brand red accent not in palette" needs semantic understanding of "red accent" — pure regex requires exact phrase variants. Tile-4's "MS Vesta row and 'Live overname' status text collide" mixes overflow, collision, AND z-order in one sentence; the 3-fix cap collapses them. The 18 FIX findings likely collapse to ~8-10 unique fixes, not 18.
4. **The 18 FIX finding accuracy is asserted, not verified.** No inter-rater reliability check; no ground-truth adjudication. A wrongly-FIX tile (audit false positive) gets repaired needlessly; a wrongly-SHIP tile (audit false negative — the subtle-clip case in vision-audit-benchmark.md §3) is never repaired. A4 has no audit-correction mechanism; REQ-006 only BLOCKS the wrong-SHIP from being adopted, it does not REPAIR it.
5. **T0/T1 A/B is structurally weak — it tests prompt structure, not the value of round-2 itself.** Both arms are GLM round-2. A proper A/B is 3 arms: T0 (raw MiniMax issue verbatim), T1 (typed fix-JSON), T2 (fresh round-1 with distilled rules). T0 vs. T1 alone cannot answer "is the typed JSON doing the work, or is any round-2-with-feedback doing it?" — this is the **single most important question** the proposal punts on.
6. **MiniMax rescore is the adopt_if signal but has unmeasured variance.** GLM is at temp 0.4 (`gen-tile.mjs:30`); MiniMax rescore temperature is unspecified. A real fix may score differently on rescore from inter-call noise, not actual regression. The `false_fix_rate ≤ 1/18` criterion (5.5%) is meaningless without a rescore-variance baseline.
7. **Open questions in spec.md §7 are unanswered in plan.md.** "Should A4 run on borderline SHIP?" and "If marginal is +1-3, is 1.3-1.5x latency justified?" are the two highest-leverage questions in the whole spec, and the plan punts both. The plan's Definition of Done ("T1 beats T0") is also non-decisional if both arms fail equally.
8. **The "9 hard invariants" partly duplicate the round-1 contract** (560×480, bottom-left title, palette, 21px weight). Re-asserting them does not address RC-3's instruction-density root cause; it amplifies it. A4 will likely make round-2 compliance *worse* on dense tiles.
9. **The "max 3 fixes per tile" cap is unjustified.** Why 3? Audit JSON shows tiles have 1-2 distinct issues typically. If the cap is a real budget, it should be 2. If it's a safety bound against prompt bloat, it should be justified by GLM's context window math (24000 tokens / ~500 per fix ≈ 48 fixes possible).
10. **The "free 2D absolute positioning forbidden" rule in the round-2 prompt is the real prize** — but it should be a phase-001 prompt-level invariant, not a per-tile fix. A4 is patching a round-1 prompt defect, not solving the primitive problem. Phase 004 (A5/A7 skeleton) addresses this properly; A4 is a temporary band-aid.

## Strongest improvement or alternative
**Drop the adapter layer and run a 3-arm T0/T1/T2 A/B on the 18 FIX tiles:**
- **T0** = round-2 with the raw MiniMax `issue` strings verbatim + the original round-1 contract
- **T1** = round-2 with typed fix-JSON (the current A4)
- **T2** = fresh round-1 with the original contract + a 10-line "lessons learned" block distilled from the 18 issues
- Same harness, same `adopt_if` gates, same cost ceiling (~1.4x baseline)

If T0 ≈ T1, the typed-JSON structure is decorative — adopt T0, save the schema overhead. If T1 ≫ T2, the typed feedback earns its place. If T2 ≈ T1, the whole A4 phase is unnecessary — a single augmented round-1 prompt regen gets the same result at lower latency, lower cost, and no MiniMax round-2 dependency. **This single test answers the open questions spec.md §7 punts on, replaces the underspecified T0/T1 design, and pre-empts the "is A4 even needed" question before ~3 weeks of implementation.**

## One thing to test or verify before building this phase
**Run one canonical FIX tile (accountbeheer-4, score 35, the textbook overflow case) through 3 round-2 prompts — T0 (raw issue text verbatim: "matrix content spills past the inner panel and overlaps bottom title/description"), T1 (typed fix-JSON with `defect_type: vertical_overflow`, `preferred_primitives: [stacked-list, linear-flow, bounded-lanes]`), T2 (fresh round-1 with one added line: "the diagram must end at or above y=348px; reserve the bottom 104px for title") — and visually compare the three outputs.** The decision threshold: **if T0 and T1 both still produce a 2D-matrix that overflows, the whole phase is dead on arrival** — GLM's primitive choice is not prompt-controllable, the 5-7 conversion prediction is wishful, and A4 should collapse into a phase-001 prompt-level invariant before any 3-arm A/B. If T0 fails but T1 succeeds, the typed structure is the value. If T2 matches T1, the whole adapter is unnecessary. **This 1-tile pilot costs ~3 GLM calls + ~30 minutes and prevents a 3-week dead-end on a 1.5-2x cost multiplier.**
